title: O Week Lecture
body:
23/12/2024




    Social Entrepreneurship Practicum (Comm3030)


                            Lecture: 0 week

     Lecturer in Charge:                 Dr Douglas Long

     Email:                              douglas.long@unsw.edu.au




1




       We are condensing a 10-week term into 5 intensive weeks, so there will be high pressure from the
        outset. It is vital that everyone is fully committed to what we are doing and that you are willing
               and able to work cooperatively in lectures, workshops, and out-of-class activities.




2




                                                                                                                     1
                                                                                               23/12/2024




                  Our focus this term will be on “St John Ambulance” as a living case study




3




    Course Objectives

    COMM3030 is a practicum course designed to integrate theory
    with experiential practice and involve authentic engagement
    with partner organisations.

    There are two key objectives:
    1. To demonstrate the importance and value of social
       enterprises in addressing social barriers, and
    2. To facilitate the development of skills for supporting effective
       social enterprise initiatives no matter the organisation for
       which one works.



4




                                                                                                      2
                                                                                                                       23/12/2024




    Weekly Course Format

               1 x 1 hour lecture weekly (on campus)
               Tuesday 10AM – 11-00 AM; before related workshop


               1-2 essential readings weekly
               Deepen understanding of core concepts

               1 x 3-hour workshop weekly (on campus)
               Activity based, philosophy to practice, design thinking

               Client meetings throughout term
               Generally held during workshop time

               Project briefs are worked on in groups so expect to meet outside of fixed class time.




5

                                                                                                                              3
                                                                                                               23/12/2024




    Course Schedule
    Week “0”           General background information on podcast

    Week 1             Introduction to Social Impact & Social Enterprise,
                       Wicked Problems and Systems Thinking

    Week 2             Client Briefings

    Week 3             Design Thinking

    Week 4             Client Feedback on initial presentations

    Week 5             Change, Logic and Leadership


7




    Facilitation Team
     Douglas Long

     In 1970, while living in a country town, I became aware that
     there were no local services available to help those who
     were in need - neither local religious or civic groups
     seemed to see this as an area of concern. Together with
     the senior local Police Officer and the Mayor, I was
     instrumental in establishing a volunteer-driven
     organisation that provided services ranging from being a
     support person in Court appearances, through budgetary
     and related advice, to assistance in finding employment
     and for interactions with government agencies.

     For more than 25 years I have been providing advice and
     support to 3 social enterprise organisations in Sydney and
     the NSW Central Coast and, in 2020, I became a non-
     executive director of a NFP startup providing mental health services in South Australia. I am a Fellow
     of the Higher Education Association.

     I am passionate about social issues and treating every person with unconditional respect. A key
     application of unconditional respect relates to the effective provision of social enterprise services.
